professor_summary: Professor Duane Griffin is described as a quirky but generally nice and smart instructor who is willing to help students, especially if they attend office hours and engage with him. Exams focus on broad trends and general connections rather than detailed memorization, though he is noted to be a picky grader. Some students appreciate his laid-back style, humor, and willingness to support those who stay on top of assignments, while others strongly caution against taking his class due to unexpected low grades and dissatisfaction with his grading fairness. Overall, success in his course appears to depend on active effort, taking advantage of extra credit, and maintaining good communication with him.
